findings.reason: 3 of 4 obligation(s) apply to the firm (jurisdiction + business-line scope)
2 applicable obligation(s) lack policy/control coverage in the inventory
1 applicable obligation(s) have no named owner in the inventory
effective_date is future (23d lead) and not retroactive

conflicts_with.note: State deletion right may require earlier purge than the federal retention floor; obtain a legal reading.

open_questions: Confirm the authoritative text and effective date against the primary source before acting.
Confirm business-line applicability and any exemptions with the accountable owner.
For each mapping gap, decide whether a new/updated policy or control is required (route to gap analysis).
For any authority conflict, obtain a legal/compliance reading on which requirement governs.
Record the implementation decision, owner, and target date; a human adjudicates disposition and closure.
disclaimer: Impact assessment and evidence only; not a compliance determination. Applicability, disposition, and closure require human adjudication. No regulatory decision, filing, or system-of-record change has been made.
narrative: Impact assessment of change RC-2026-0142 (CFPB, 12 CFR Part 1033, effective 2026-08-10; 23-day lead as of 2026-07-18). Three of four obligations appear in scope for the firm on jurisdiction and business-line criteria (OB-1 data-access interface, OB-2 recordkeeping, OB-3 recipient disclosure); OB-4 (broker-dealer supervision) does not overlap the firm profile. Two applicable obligations lack policy/control coverage in the inventory (OB-2 missing a control, OB-3 missing both a policy and a control), OB-3 has no named owner, the effective date is within the near-term window, and OB-2 declares a conflict with a state deletion right that must be read by legal. Recommended disposition: Priority. A human must confirm applicability and scoping, resolve the conflict, close the mapping gaps, and adjudicate disposition and closure. Impact assessment and evidence only; not a compliance determination. Applicability, disposition, and closure require human adjudication. No regulatory decision, filing, or system-of-record change has been made.
